title: serianthes rurutensis
intro: Serianthes rurutensis, also known as the Raratongan rosewood or mountain rosewood, is a species of flowering plant in the legume family, Fabaceae. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, due to its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antibacterial properties. The health benefits of Serianthes rurutensis include reducing inflammation, improving digestion, and providing relief from respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma. Herbal preparations of Serianthes rurutensis include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which can be made by steeping the leaves and bark in hot water or a solvent like ethanol.
